---
title: "About"
---

<div class="page">

I'm a postdoc at the [Vrije Universiteit Brussel](https://www.vub.be) (VUB) in Belgium, part of the [HYDR](https://hydr.vub.be) research group and the [bglacier](https://hydr.vub.be/research-groups/bglacier) glaciology group, working with Prof. Harry Zekollari.

Growing up in Chile, I've always been drawn to mountains and water. That fascination led me to study how climate change is reshaping the glaciers and rivers of Patagonia — and increasingly, mountain regions around the world. I combine physics-based models with machine learning to tackle questions about glacier evolution, water security, and what a warmer future means for the people and ecosystems that depend on mountain water.

## Background

I did my **PhD in Environmental Sciences** at the Universidad de Concepción (Chile, 2023), where I studied how climate change alters the freshwater flowing into the coastal fjords of Western Patagonia. During my PhD, I spent seven months at the **University of Innsbruck** working with Fabien Maussion on regional glacier modelling — a collaboration that sparked my love for glacier model development.

Before that, I earned a **Master's in Civil Engineering** (Water Resources) and a **Bachelor's in Civil Engineering** (Hydraulic-Environmental), both from the Universidad de Concepción.

## What I'm working on

- **ICE-BUFFER** — My FWO-funded postdoctoral project: using deep learning to understand how glaciers buffer streamflow droughts globally as the climate changes
- **Hybrid glacio-hydrological modelling** — Bridging glacier and hydrological models to improve water projections in Patagonia and other mountain regions

</div>
